description: Frequent lightweight transactions on a hot partition serialize through Paxos and collapse throughput.
body: Failure mode: routine writes use IF conditions or IF NOT EXISTS on a hot key, so each runs a multi-round Paxos consensus and contends with the others on the same partition, causing latency to spike and writes to time out under load. Detection signals: CAS/Paxos contention metrics climb, LWT operations dominate the write mix, and timeouts cluster on one partition. Do not use LWT for high-throughput writes. Safer fallback: model the operation as an idempotent write with a natural key so no compare-and-set is needed, reserve LWT for genuinely rare uniqueness conflicts, and measure that the LWT rate stays a small fraction of writes.
